AUDIT-REGISTRY-001.title: Stamp every cleanup pass in the audit registry
AUDIT-REGISTRY-001.instruction: Refresh file inventory before broad work, then stamp touched files and pass proof before finalizing.
sunset_when: The registry update is enforced by automation or CI
Three consecutive cleanup passes include complete pass receipts

sunset_review.note: meta-gate automates registry integrity; pass-stamping discipline remains manual

DOC-HYGIENE-001.title: Prefer versioned owners over new markdown sprawl
DOC-HYGIENE-001.instruction: Update an existing source-of-truth doc or registry entry before creating a new doc. Mark stale snapshots historical instead of letting conflicting instructions accumulate. Governed document versions may increase or remain stable but must never decrease or silently lose their catalog identity/path.
sunset_when: Docs index and doc_versions remain current for five broad passes

EXHIBIT-FRESHNESS-001.title: Keep architecture exhibits fresh against their tracker anchors
EXHIBIT-FRESHNESS-001.instruction: Every architecture reference exhibit must declare an exhibit-freshness marker, point to its tracker source, keep the tracker architectureExhibit anchor in sync, and remove stale code snippets when the reference implementation changes.
sunset_when: Architecture exhibit blocks are generated from source markers or another stronger source-backed mechanism

AGENT-HARNESS-001.title: Use the agent harness for broad AI-assisted work
AGENT-HARNESS-001.instruction: Before broad cleanup, refactor, docs consolidation, design parity, or workflow-harness work, generate a scoped context pack, check matching regression guards, run the agent readiness gate, and record proof or blockers in the audit registry. Add durable failure modes to docs/agent_regression_ledger.json instead of relying on thread memory.
sunset_when: The agent readiness gate is mandatory in CI for broad-work branches
Five consecutive broad passes include context-pack proof, readiness proof, and no missing regression-ledger updates

AGENT-DELEGATION-001.title: Keep parallel agent work parent-integrated and worktree-isolated
AGENT-DELEGATION-001.instruction: When using subagents for speed, isolate their work in short-lived Git worktrees/branches, assign disjoint write scopes, keep canonical docs/generated registries/audit receipts parent-owned by default, review commits before importing, run final verification in the parent branch, and record a delegation outcome metric.
sunset_when: Delegation outcome metrics show five consecutive accepted batches with no file-ownership conflicts
The readiness gate validates delegation protocol coverage and metrics shape

WEB-COPY-001.title: Keep marketing-authored website copy in the content layer
WEB-COPY-001.instruction: User-visible marketing-authored strings belong under website/src/content. Existing component strings are temporary baseline debt; do not add new entries. Move strings into page-specific content modules and shrink the baseline as pages migrate. Permanent technical exceptions require an exact-string allowlist entry with a reason.
sunset_when: The website copy baseline is empty and remains empty for five copy changes

sunset_review.note: The recovered baseline is empty, but this rule remains the regression guard that keeps marketing-authored strings in the content layer.

WEB-ROUTE-001.title: Keep marketing website route contracts ahead of visual review
WEB-ROUTE-001.instruction: Before changing public marketing routes, generated organizer route families, SEO metadata, static postbuild output, robots/sitemap behavior, review states, route Storybook evidence, or route-owned URL state, update design/website/routes.json when the public contract changes and pass node tool/run.mjs check marketing:website-routes plus node tool/run.mjs check web:react-architecture-boundaries when React route boundaries are touched. The route gate validates runtime/static metadata, rejects deployable organizer listings outside live active-market cities, and validates matching Storybook parameters.catchRoute declarations for Storybook-backed review states. Start component-first review only after the route contract is current.
sunset_when: A website component workbench enforces route-linked page/state coverage in CI
Five consecutive marketing website route changes pass the route contract gate without follow-up fixes

WEB-DEPENDENCY-TOPOLOGY-001.title: Keep React dependency topology deterministic and surface-safe
WEB-DEPENDENCY-TOPOLOGY-001.instruction: Keep the checked React dependency graph current. Repo-local imports must resolve, website and admin may share through governed packages rather than importing each other directly, and current strongly connected components remain visible debt rather than hidden by an allowlist. Run node tool/web/react_dependency_graph.mjs --check whenever React imports or workspace exports move.
sunset_when: Never; deterministic dependency topology is a permanent architecture contract

WEB-UI-PRIMITIVE-001.title: React interactive UI must use shared primitives
WEB-UI-PRIMITIVE-001.instruction: React feature, app, and story code must not render native interactive elements such as button, anchor, input, select, or textarea directly. Route those elements through the shared UI primitive owner for the surface, or add a temporary react-ui-primitive-allow override with a stable debt id and removal plan. Run node tool/run.mjs check web:react-ui-primitives before handoff.
sunset_when: The React primitive scanner is enforced in CI for website and admin
The remaining form/table/layout component families have equivalent registry-backed scanners

WEB-UI-COMPONENT-001.title: React governed component families must use shared wrappers
WEB-UI-COMPONENT-001.instruction: React feature, app, and story code must route governed component families through shared primitives instead of rendering the shell directly. Marketing website exported uppercase feature components must be declared in design/website/components.json or made private, so route/section/supporting components remain registry-backed. Admin feature UI files may export route/workspace entry components such as *Screen and *Workspace; reusable panels, cards, lists, badges, and sections belong in admin/src/shared/ui or stay private. Admin route/workspace entries, shared admin primitives, and admin feedback providers must be declared in design/admin/components.json. Cross-surface primitive overlap and @catch/web-ui exports must stay classified in docs/audit_registry/web_shared_primitive_adoption.json. Do not pass className directly outside shared UI/site primitive owner files; move the style decision into a primitive or use a temporary react-classname-boundary-allow override with a stable debt id and removal plan. The canonical governed-family registry is generated by `node tool/web/check_react_component_governance.mjs --families-json` and checked in at `docs/audit_registry/react_component_governance_families.json`. The family scanner is a known-family blocklist: passing it does not classify novel component shells, so repeated new shell drift must become a scanner family before handoff. Run `node tool/run.mjs check marketing:website-components`, `node tool/run.mjs check web:admin-feature-exports`, `node tool/run.mjs check web:admin-components`, `node tool/run.mjs check web:react-classname-boundaries`, `node tool/run.mjs check web:react-component-governance`, and `node tool/run.mjs check web:shared-ui-adoption` before handoff.
sunset_when: The React component-governance scanner is enforced in CI for website and admin
Form groups, filters, nav rails, layout shells, metric cards, and status blocks have equivalent registry-backed scanners or documented non-governed classifications

WEB-QUERY-STATE-001.title: React server-state pending state must ratchet toward TanStack Query
WEB-QUERY-STATE-001.instruction: React feature controllers and feature use* hooks should not add new manual loading, saving, submitting, pending, or in-flight state for server reads or mutations. Use TanStack Query query/mutation state and explicit query-key invalidation for server state; keep only view-local form, filter, selection, and client-only rendering state in React state. Run node tool/run.mjs check web:react-query-state before handoff.
sunset_when: The React query-state baseline is empty for website and admin feature controllers/hooks
New React server-state work consistently uses query keys, mutation pending state, and explicit invalidation without manual async-state flags

CODEGEN-001.title: Keep generated Dart outputs synchronized during iterative work
CODEGEN-001.instruction: When editing Riverpod, Freezed, json_serializable, envied, or other build_runner-backed Dart sources, keep generated outputs from build_runner instead of manually reverting hash/output churn. Prefer running `dart run build_runner watch --delete-conflicting-outputs` during iterative cleanup loops, and stop it only when the user asks or when the session no longer needs codegen.
sunset_when: Code generation is enforced by CI or a repository-local dev daemon

FORM-CONTRACT-001.title: Editable form constraints derive from schema contracts
FORM-CONTRACT-001.instruction: Editable consumer and host form rows that map to schema-owned fields must bind the generated CatchContractFieldConstraints entry instead of repeating length, required, pattern, enum, or numeric constraints as literals.
sunset_when: All schema-backed form factories are generated directly from schema-owned form metadata

SCHEMA-FIXTURE-001.title: Contract fixtures stay schema-backed
SCHEMA-FIXTURE-001.instruction: Every JSON fixture under contracts/fixtures/valid or contracts/fixtures/invalid must be declared in tool/contracts/check_schema_fixtures.mjs and must validate with the expected pass/fail result against its generated JSON Schema.
sunset_when: Fixture-schema mapping is generated from contract metadata or embedded fixture metadata

MIGRATION-CONTRACT-001.title: Migration contracts keep phase evidence machine-checkable
MIGRATION-CONTRACT-001.instruction: Every migration contract must declare schemaVersion, logicalName, currentPhase, reason, unique phases with known statuses, operational evidence such as guards or known consumers, and liveApply evidence when any phase is complete_remote.
sunset_when: Migration metadata is generated from a stronger typed migration registry

STORAGE-CONTRACT-001.title: Storage path contracts stay tied to rules and rules tests
STORAGE-CONTRACT-001.instruction: Every storage path schema must declare x-storage-rules-match, x-storage-read, x-storage-write, x-storage-rules-test-file, and matching storage.rules coverage. Review passes for storage contracts should run the static storage checker and, when feasible, the firestore/storage emulator rules suite.
sunset_when: Storage path contracts and storage.rules are generated from one typed registry

STORAGE-IAM-001.title: Storage rules cross-service dependencies stay provisioned and live-probed
STORAGE-IAM-001.instruction: Storage rules that call firestore.get or firestore.exists require roles/firebaserules.firestoreServiceAgent on every environment's Firebase Storage service agent. Storage deploys must fail their IAM preflight when it is absent, and authenticated upload/delete behavior must remain live-probeable without weakening participant rules.
sunset_when: Firebase provisions and continuously verifies cross-service rules IAM automatically

sunset_review.note: The check and canary enforce the dependency, but Firebase still does not provision or continuously verify it for this project.

FIRESTORE-RULES-TEST-001.title: Run Firebase rules tests under the required emulators
FIRESTORE-RULES-TEST-001.instruction: Do not run the Firebase rules suite as a standalone Node/npm test unless the required emulators are already running. From the repo root, prefer `firebase emulators:exec --only firestore,storage "npm --prefix functions run test:rules"`. Treat `connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8080` or `connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:9199` as an emulator-workflow failure first, not proof that the rules or tests are broken.
sunset_when: A wrapper script or CI-only command makes the emulator requirement impossible to bypass
Functions README and data-contract tracker stay in sync for three rules passes

DESIGN-PRIMITIVES-001.title: Prefer shared design primitives over repeated local shells
DESIGN-PRIMITIVES-001.instruction: Before creating local card, sheet, empty-state, rail, row, badge, skeleton, or section UI, check docs/widget_catalog.md and the existing Catch* primitives under lib/core/widgets for an owner. Do not duplicate an existing primitive shell; extend the primitive or propose a variant through catalog review. Section titles have one owner: top-level groups route through CatchSection, contained card titles route through CatchSection.contained or a documented content primitive, and nested shared widgets must expose a content-only mode instead of repeating the parent section title. Underline tab/scope rails route through CatchTabRail or CatchOptionGroup; production code must not compose CatchOptionGroupItem directly because that forks divider, indicator, ink, and trailing-action alignment behavior.
sunset_when: Widget catalog shows no repeated local shells outside intentional feature-specific UI

WIDGET-PATTERN-FAMILY-001.title: Consolidate UI through reviewed pattern families, not raw similarity pairs
WIDGET-PATTERN-FAMILY-001.instruction: Generated similarity pairs and clusters are discovery evidence only. Assign candidates to a stable pattern family, render every available member side by side, choose the strongest visual/API reference, and record per-member canonical, repair, unify, register, or discard dispositions plus accepted visual deltas before creating a work order. Apply K/R/D rules only as post-decision implementation tactics, then rerender and prove the full family.
sunset_when: Every actionable widget-consolidation candidate is assigned to a stable reviewed family or an explicit cross-family boundary

ASYNC-UI-001.title: Async presentation states use shared boundaries and content-shaped skeletons
ASYNC-UI-001.instruction: Presentation widgets should route AsyncValue, Future, and Stream loading/error branches through CatchAsyncValueView, CatchAsyncValueSliver, or a feature-owned typed display-state adapter. View-models may call AsyncValue.when when they return non-widget display state. Empty states stay in successful data callbacks. Data-load errors must use Catch error primitives with retry unless retry is impossible or unsafe.
sunset_when: Analyzer or scanner coverage enforces raw async UI branches and retryable data-load errors

ASYNC-ENRICHMENT-001.title: Optional enrichment failures stay non-primary
ASYNC-ENRICHMENT-001.instruction: Optional enrichment reads may fail silently only when the primary surface remains correct and no primary CTA, paid action, safety action, or host operation depends on the enrichment. Silent enrichment failures should still log through error context when diagnosis matters. Anything a primary action depends on is not enrichment and must surface its error.
sunset_when: Optional enrichment failures are inventoried separately from primary action failures for three app architecture passes

ASYNC-LOADING-PRESENTATION-001.title: Async loading states render credible data before blocking skeletons and always recover
ASYNC-LOADING-PRESENTATION-001.instruction: When credible initial or fallback data is available (route extra, cached value, optimistic snapshot), render it ahead of blocking loading skeletons. Loading states should use skeletons that resemble the eventual UI shape when that shape is known; use compact spinners or loading copy only for tiny inline metadata, unknown one-off work, or platform/plugin actions. Initial provider loads must reach an actionable error state after the shared presentation deadline, and CatchAsyncValueView/CatchAsyncValueSliver call sites must supply onRetry that restarts the failing provider boundary.
sunset_when: Widgetbook and captures include content-shaped loading states for every contracted screen

WIDGET-CATALOG-001.title: Keep widget catalog current when widget architecture changes
WIDGET-CATALOG-001.instruction: Update docs/widget_catalog.md in the same pass when adding, deleting, moving, renaming, or materially changing a widget, primitive API, screen ownership model, sliver/tab structure, or reusable design-system role. Do not update the catalog for tiny implementation-only edits that do not change inventory or usage guidance.
sunset_when: A generated widget inventory or CI check makes catalog drift visible automatically

DESIGN-TOKENS-001.title: Keep design-system ownership centralized
DESIGN-TOKENS-001.instruction: Theme tokens, typography, app theme, spacing, radius, motion, icon sizes, and compatibility layout helpers should live under lib/core/theme. Do not add new design primitives under generic lib/constants or a parallel top-level lib/theme folder.
sunset_when: Legacy spacing compatibility usage is either migrated or intentionally documented

UI-LINT-001.title: Codify audited UI invariants as analyzer plugin rules
UI-LINT-001.instruction: When a UI surface invariant is deterministic, move it into packages/catch_ui_lints as an analysis_server_plugin diagnostic that runs across handwritten lib/** by default. Verify Catch diagnostics with the seeded harness or repository-root dart analyze, never flutter analyze or a targeted subdirectory analysis. Generate steering tables from component contracts, use the resolved checker for cross-file composition, exempt only semantic source-of-truth folders or generated code, and keep broad scanners only for triage and migration discovery.
sunset_when: All high-value UI scanner findings either migrate into analyzer plugin diagnostics or are documented as intentionally broad triage queues
New audited screens stay covered by strict UI diagnostics without custom one-off scripts

SCREEN-GUTTER-001.title: Screen gutters use semantic body and section ownership
SCREEN-GUTTER-001.instruction: Screen, page, and body horizontal gutters should be owned by CatchInsets roles, CatchScreenBody, CatchSectionStack, CatchSection, CatchDetailSliverSectionList, or another documented layout primitive. Raw EdgeInsets rebuilt from CatchSpacing.screenPx/s5 in presentation code is a review candidate unless it is clearly component-local geometry.
sunset_when: High-confidence screen-gutter candidates reach zero or are cataloged as accepted local geometry
Deterministic cases graduate into analyzer diagnostics or a failing ratchet

SCREEN-CHROME-001.title: Every screen declares one reviewed navigation-chrome owner
SCREEN-CHROME-001.instruction: Every handwritten Scaffold appBar must have an exact reviewed role in the screen-top-bar manifest: root or root-like screen voice uses CatchScreenTopBar, compact detail/edit/utility navigation uses CatchTopBar, and identity navigation uses CatchTopBar.identity. Pushed routes that require an exit declare leading back and must configure an explicit back affordance. Raw Material or Cupertino navigation bars require an exact durable media-hero exception. Feature-owned Header and TopBar classes must be classified by symbol and owner, and full-screen editors that cover shell chrome must present through the root navigator. Broad file allowlists and unrelated canonical calls elsewhere in a file do not satisfy the contract.
sunset_when: Every remaining legacy manual-header entry has migrated to its canonical screen, step-flow, workspace, or content owner
The analyzer can enforce exact app-bar role and route-presentation ownership without a manifest

RESPONSIVE-LAYOUT-001.title: Avoid hardcoded widget dimensions for content-sized UI
RESPONSIVE-LAYOUT-001.instruction: Treat fixed heights and widths on content-bearing widgets as a layout smell. Prefer intrinsic content sizing, flexible constraints, aspect ratios for truly fixed-format media, and min/max constraints only when they preserve responsiveness across screen sizes, text scale, localization, and variable backend content. Fixed dimensions are acceptable for icons, avatars, progress indicators, toolbars, known-format controls, and intentionally bounded visual assets, but cards, tiles, list rows, and sections should not depend on magic-number heights to look correct.
sunset_when: Widget cleanup scans classify fixed dimensions by acceptable fixed-format controls versus content-sized UI
Dashboard, run-club, chat, profile, and run-detail passes have removed unjustified fixed content-card heights

DENORMALIZED-DATA-001.title: Keep denormalized projection fields in sync
DENORMALIZED-DATA-001.instruction: Whenever source-of-truth profile, run, club, membership, participation, match, review, or notification data is denormalized into another document, the write path must identify the source field, projection fields, and sync mechanism. Prefer backend triggers or callable-owned fan-out over client-side best effort. Add tests proving projection updates when the source changes, and add repair/backfill tooling for existing drift when needed.
sunset_when: A projection inventory lists all denormalized fields and their sync owner
CI or a data audit job checks projection drift for critical collections

DEBUG-LOOP-001.title: Use interactive phone debug logs as feedback evidence
DEBUG-LOOP-001.instruction: When user is clicking through the app on a physical phone, run an interactive flutter run session through tool/flutter_with_env.sh, keep stdin open for reload/restart, and summarize fresh logs in pass proof.
sunset_when: A stable automated device-smoke workflow replaces manual debug sessions

CONTROLLER-BOUNDARY-001.title: Keep product behavior out of presentation widgets
CONTROLLER-BOUNDARY-001.instruction: Widgets may own focus, scroll, animations, navigation, temporary input state, and rendering. Move repository writes, repository-provider reads, validation decisions, mutation orchestration, product side effects, and WidgetRef-threaded provider access into controllers, view-model providers, or repositories.
sunset_when: Presentation repository-provider scanner stays clean for five cleanup passes

DEPENDENCY-DIRECTION-001.title: Layer imports follow dependency direction
DEPENDENCY-DIRECTION-001.instruction: Layer imports follow the declared dependency direction. lib/*/domain/** must not import Flutter, Riverpod, Firebase, routing, or platform plugin packages; Timestamp/serialization handling routes through core/firestore_converters, and the existing direct imports are ratcheted debt in tool/architecture/dependency_direction_baseline.json — new domain code must not add them. lib/*/data/** and lib/*/domain/** must not import feature presentation code. Feature presentation must not import sibling feature presentation internals. Sanctioned seams are (a) a sibling feature public controller (presentation/*_controller.dart) imported from a route screen (*_screen.dart) or another controller, and (b) symbols exported by the sibling feature root barrel (lib/<feature>/<feature>.dart), where every presentation export in a barrel requires a // public-api: annotation. Direct deep imports of sibling presentation files remain violations. Widgets, bodies, state adapters, and view models are feature internals — when another feature needs a read model, expose a narrow public provider/contract or annotated barrel seam instead.
sunset_when: The dependency-direction baseline is empty and adopted architecture boundary checks cover aligned features

PROVIDER-TOPOLOGY-001.title: Keep the static Riverpod topology current and reviewed
PROVIDER-TOPOLOGY-001.instruction: Generate the complete static Riverpod source graph from Dart ASTs. Keep providers, families, consumers, aliases, overrides, and Mutations resolvable; reject duplicate or dangling providers, provider-internal unresolved refs, reactive cycles, stale artifacts, unreviewed architecture candidates, and stale review decisions. Core-owned providers must not orchestrate feature data, and provider aliases should be removed when the canonical owner can be used directly.
sunset_when: An equally deterministic compiler-owned graph export replaces the repo generator and preserves the same coverage and reviewed-candidate gate

sunset_review.note: The repo generator now enforces the topology, but it remains the active source of truth until Riverpod exposes an equally complete and deterministic compiler-owned graph export.

PROVIDER-SEAM-001.title: Use feature-owned provider seams
PROVIDER-SEAM-001.instruction: If a feature has a view model, controller, or feature-specific provider, screens should use that seam rather than reaching around it to a lower-level repository provider.
sunset_when: Feature screens consistently read view models/controllers and no scanner reaches remain

ACTION-CARDINALITY-001.title: Declare action cardinality before wiring UI affordances
ACTION-CARDINALITY-001.instruction: Every user-visible action must declare its scope key and cardinality: not allowed, singleton, unbounded, or domain-bounded. Singleton actions must render their resulting state instead of duplicate CTAs and be enforced server-side. Unbounded actions must have a UI that supports zero, one, and many items. Domain bounds must be real product constraints, not UI shortcuts.
sunset_when: The action inventory covers all primary callables and CTAs
New feature specs include action cardinality as a required field

PENDING-SNAPSHOT-001.title: Protect the submitted request snapshot while an operation is pending
PENDING-SNAPSHOT-001.instruction: Every pending user operation must declare one integrity policy. Frozen snapshots disable request-defining inputs, conflicting actions, mutation-reset callbacks, and unsafe route exits until settlement. Versioned snapshots require immutable generations plus stale-result rejection or cancellation and tests. Independently keyed concurrency requires distinct typed scope keys and separate pending/error surfaces. A loading button alone is not sufficient; controllers must also deduplicate duplicate dispatches or use an equivalent idempotency boundary.
sunset_when: Never; pending request integrity remains a permanent cross-runtime contract

EVENT-POLICY-INDEV-001.title: Preserve event policy engine through production migration
EVENT-POLICY-INDEV-001.instruction: Treat lib/event_policies as an intentional in-development feature under production migration, not dead code. New event creation and booking may write/read EventPolicyBundle snapshots, but the dev lab must remain read-only and the migration must stay backward-compatible with legacy capacity, price, and EventConstraints documents until the schema migration is complete.
sunset_when: The event policy engine fully replaces legacy event capacity, price, and EventConstraints fields
Or the product decision explicitly cancels the event-policy migration

EVENT-SUCCESS-INDEV-001.title: Preserve parallel event success layer until product decision
EVENT-SUCCESS-INDEV-001.instruction: Treat lib/event_success as an intentional in-development production rollout, not dead code. It may be wired into live event routes through controller/repository seams, but new live modules need explicit privacy, safety, backend ownership, and Firestore read/write behavior in the same pass. Private-crush target identities stay attendee-private; host reporting may use aggregate metrics only.
sunset_when: The event success layer is fully production-owned and no longer needs an in-development preservation marker
Or the product decision explicitly cancels the live event success layer

STREAM-LIFECYCLE-001.title: Do not idle-timeout realtime Firestore listeners
STREAM-LIFECYCLE-001.instruction: Do not wrap Firestore snapshot streams in Stream.timeout to detect stalled loads; realtime streams may legitimately stay silent after initial data. Prefer Riverpod autoDispose, screen-active gating, explicit refresh/retry UI, and focused tests that advance fake time past prior timeout thresholds. Every keepAlive provider must carry a // keepalive: rationale marker within the three lines above the annotation or declaration.
sunset_when: A scanner prevents Stream.timeout usage on repository/provider realtime listeners
All retained stream timeouts are documented as non-Firestore protocol deadlines

sunset_review.note: dependency scanner covers data stream timeouts; rule stays active until retained protocol deadlines are reviewed and the ratchet stays clean

ERROR-UI-001.title: Use branded error surfaces for app-facing failures
ERROR-UI-001.instruction: Do not add new raw Center(Text(...)) full-screen errors or reintroduce CatchErrorText-style compatibility wrappers. Route app-facing data-load failures through CatchErrorState, CatchErrorScaffold, CatchSliverErrorState, or CatchInlineErrorState with appErrorMessage/appErrorTitle mapped copy. Every full-screen or sliver error must expose onRetry or a truthful secondary action such as CatchErrorBackAction; inline errors may inherit recovery from their surrounding section. Keep CatchFrameworkErrorView for Flutter framework crashes only, ErrorBanner for inline mutation/form failures, and showCatchErrorSnackBar for transient action failures.
sunset_when: A canonical app error primitive covers full-screen, sliver, and inline contexts
Existing AsyncValue error branches have migrated away from raw centered text except where explicitly justified

ERROR-CATALOG-001.title: Keep the app error catalogue current
ERROR-CATALOG-001.instruction: When adding, renaming, or removing an AppException subclass, stable error code, error mapper, AppErrorContext, branded error primitive, or repeated app-facing failure category, update the error handling contract and error quick-reference sections in docs/app_architecture.md in the same pass. Add typed exceptions only for stable product/domain errors that need branching, analytics, retry policy, or tests; use mapped copy for one-off message changes.
sunset_when: A generated or CI-backed error catalogue check validates subclass/code/doc parity

FLOW-MUTATION-001.title: Advance multi-step flows only after required mutations succeed
FLOW-MUTATION-001.instruction: In multi-step controllers and widgets, do not change the durable flow step or navigate past the current step before the required repository write or external mutation succeeds, unless the transition is intentionally optimistic and has explicit rollback/error UI.
sunset_when: Controller tests cover failure paths for all multi-step flow mutations
No flow controller advances state before awaited persistence without an explicit optimistic contract

EXTERNAL-SIDE-EFFECT-001.title: Put plugin and platform side effects behind seams
EXTERNAL-SIDE-EFFECT-001.instruction: Store launches, URL launches, share sheets, image pickers, connectivity subscriptions, FCM setup, and other platform/plugin effects should sit behind providers/controllers/services so tests can replace them.
sunset_when: Presentation plugin/platform import scanner remains clean for five passes

sunset_review.note: dependency scanner blocks presentation plugin imports; broader side-effect seams still need manual architecture review

TOKEN-DRILLING-001.title: Avoid prop drilling CatchTokens
TOKEN-DRILLING-001.instruction: Prefer CatchTokens.of(context) inside leaf widgets instead of passing CatchTokens through constructors unless intentionally rendering with a non-current token set.
sunset_when: Token prop-drilling scanner remains clean for three passes

TEST-ASYNC-001.title: Use named async test flushing helpers
TEST-ASYNC-001.instruction: Use flushTestEventQueue instead of raw Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero) when tests need queued provider stream or callback delivery.
sunset_when: Scanner reports no raw zero-delay async flushes for three passes

WIDGET-TEST-001.title: Centralize broad widget test settling
WIDGET-TEST-001.instruction: Use semantic/domain pump helpers or pumpFeatureUi instead of scattered raw pumpAndSettle calls in feature tests.
sunset_when: Only the approved centralized helper-level broad wait remains
No feature-level raw pumpAndSettle appears for three passes

WIDGET-SEMANTICS-001.title: Make custom tappables semantic before testing them
WIDGET-SEMANTICS-001.instruction: Before adding fragile widget finders, give custom tap targets stable semantics, labels, tooltips, or keys that describe real user actions.
sunset_when: Feature tappable scanner remains clean for three cleanup passes

SLIVER-001.title: Respect sliver ownership and NestedScrollView overlap contracts
SLIVER-001.instruction: If a parent owns a CustomScrollView, children should usually return slivers; NestedScrollView tabs must inject absorbed overlap or own the inner scroll view. Collapsible title headers followed by pinned bottom rows must move the title upward as it collapses; do not let sticky search/filter/tab rows visually scroll over and cover the title.
sunset_when: Sliver screens have dedicated regression tests and no known blank/overflow regressions

TAB-ROOT-SCROLL-001.title: Tab-root scroll owners clear adaptive shell chrome
TAB-ROOT-SCROLL-001.instruction: Every direct consumer and host StatefulShellBranch must declare its true root scroll owners in tool/design/tab_root_scroll_contracts.json. Root scrolls end in CatchSliverTerminalPadding or CatchScrollTerminalPadding, use SafeArea(bottom: false) when the terminal owns the device inset, consume raw floating iOS shell obstruction, and add no tab-bar inset for Android chrome already reserved by Scaffold. Box-layout terminal empty/error branches use CatchStateViewport; presentation slivers use CatchSliverStateViewport, CatchSliverEmptyState, or CatchSliverErrorState rather than composing SliverFillRemaining directly, so the same shell obstruction controls their optical center.
sunset_when: Every tab-root scroll surface is structurally created through one canonical root-scroll constructor that appends terminal clearance automatically

ERROR-UI-002.title: Data-load errors must use a Catch primitive and blocking states must expose recovery
ERROR-UI-002.instruction: AsyncValue/stream error branches must render CatchErrorState/CatchSliverErrorState/CatchInlineErrorState.fromError with mapped copy. Provider failures use onRetry that invalidates the failing provider; terminal full-screen and sliver route errors use a truthful secondary action such as CatchErrorBackAction. Reserve CatchEmptyState for genuinely-empty success states; never route an error into an empty-state or a hand-rolled card that discards the error object.
sunset_when: A catch_ui_lints diagnostic flags CatchEmptyState/bespoke cards inside error branches
No feature error branch renders raw/empty surfaces for three passes

MUTATION-KEY-GRAIN-001.title: Mutation state keys match interaction grain
MUTATION-KEY-GRAIN-001.instruction: Mutation state cardinality must match UI interaction grain. Route-level single actions (book, cancel, submit, delete for the route's single subject) may use static Mutation fields. Repeated row/list actions — attendance, waitlist offers, join-request decisions, any per-entity action rendered N times on one surface — must key mutation state per target (mutation(key) family instances) so one row's pending/error state cannot affect sibling rows. Keys are typed Dart records with value equality, not concatenated strings. Reference implementation: lib/hosts/presentation/host_event_booking_controller.dart.
sunset_when: Repeated row/list mutation actions consistently use typed record keys for three app architecture passes

MUTATION-ERROR-SURFACE-001.title: A watched Mutation consumed for isPending must also surface hasError
MUTATION-ERROR-SURFACE-001.instruction: Any UI that watches a Mutation to drive a spinner (isPending) must also handle hasError — via an inline ErrorBanner.fromError, a screen-level MutationErrorSnackbarListener, or try/catch + showCatchErrorSnackBar around .run(). Silent failure (spinner clears, no feedback) is forbidden, especially for destructive/security actions.
sunset_when: A scanner flags isPending watches with no matching hasError handling
Mutation failure paths have controller/widget tests for three passes

sunset_review.note: scanner shipped 2026-07-02; rule stays active until the per-mutation analyzer diagnostic replaces the file-level heuristic

FIRESTORE-PARTIAL-WRITE-001.title: Mutating writes use partial update()/FieldValue of changed keys, not full-document set() of a client snapshot
FIRESTORE-PARTIAL-WRITE-001.instruction: Client and callable mutating writes must update only changed keys via update()/set(merge:true)/FieldValue ops. Full-document set() of a client-held snapshot is forbidden outside a transaction-guarded create; it loses Timestamp precision, trips rules diff(), and causes last-write-wins races between independent fields.
sunset_when: read-modify-set is gone from event_success and other repositories
A scanner flags set() of a full fetched snapshot for three passes

FIRESTORE-INDEX-PARITY-001.title: Composite Firestore queries stay synchronized with deployed index configuration
FIRESTORE-INDEX-PARITY-001.instruction: Every repository-owned composite query builder declares its ordered firestore-index contract beside the query. The checked index configuration must contain every declared contract, retired collection indexes must not survive renames, and the parity gate must run before deploy.
sunset_when: The Firestore SDK or deployment platform provides equivalent compile-time query/index validation

sunset_review.note: The gate closes a production FAILED_PRECONDITION path and remains necessary while indexes are configured separately from Dart query builders.

FIRESTORE-READ-LIMIT-POLICY-001.title: Repository read limits use one reviewed surface policy
FIRESTORE-READ-LIMIT-POLICY-001.instruction: Growing repository reads use a reviewed ReadLimitPolicy value plus an opaque cursor path. Numeric limit and take literals must not be reintroduced in repository code, and bounded-set exceptions must remain explicit in data_contracts.md.
sunset_when: A generated repository layer owns page-size and cursor contracts for every Firestore read

sunset_review.note: The gate prevents the ad-hoc limit zoo from returning while cursor adoption proceeds surface by surface.

DATA-ACCESS-BATCH-001.title: List/roster surfaces resolve display-only data via one batched provider, not per-tile streams or sequential reads
DATA-ACCESS-BATCH-001.instruction: Display-only profile/event/club data for lists and rosters must be resolved through a single batched (whereIn/getAll) provider, never one realtime snapshot listener per tile or sequential per-id awaits. Share one whereIn fan-out + chunk helper across repositories.
sunset_when: Per-tile snapshot listeners and sequential per-id reads are removed from list surfaces
A shared batched-read helper is the single fan-out implementation

INJECTED-CLOCK-001.title: Time-dependent domain getters take an injected clock, not DateTime.now()
INJECTED-CLOCK-001.instruction: Domain getters/predicates with time semantics must accept an injected DateTime/referenceNow when any caller threads one, instead of calling DateTime.now() internally — otherwise adjacent branches disagree and tests cannot pin time.
sunset_when: No domain time predicate calls DateTime.now() internally where a referenceNow is threaded

sunset_review.note: dependency scanner covers domain DateTime.now access; rule stays active until existing clock debt is burned down and tests pin time seams

NO-OP-CONDITIONAL-001.title: Ternary and switch/if arms must not be structurally identical
NO-OP-CONDITIONAL-001.instruction: A conditional whose branches return/produce identical values is dead logic; remove the condition or fix the divergent branch. Often a symptom of an incomplete rename or a forgotten variant.
sunset_when: A lint flags identical-branch conditionals (dart analyzer / catch_ui_lints) and the codebase is clean for three passes

DEAD-PUBLIC-SYMBOL-001.title: Public widgets/getters/enum values in non-labs lib/ must have a production reference
DEAD-PUBLIC-SYMBOL-001.instruction: Public symbols (widgets, getters, enum values) outside lib/labs and intentional in-dev features must have at least one live reference. Live references are: production lib/ code, route/config registration, and Widgetbook use cases or capture-catalog entries for components that have a widget-catalog or component-contract entry. Test-only references do not count as live. A symbol whose only live reference is Widgetbook and which has no catalog/contract entry is dead. Orphaned symbols left by superseded migrations must be deleted together with their dead tests.
sunset_when: A dead-public-symbol scan is clean (minus the documented in-dev carve-out) for three passes

APP-COPY-001.title: Product copy is owned by typed, reviewable locale catalogs
APP-COPY-001.instruction: Handwritten user-facing Flutter copy belongs in lib/l10n/app_en.arb with description, audience, owner, and surface metadata and is consumed through generated AppLocalizations. Domain and controller layers carry semantic ids, codes, and placeholder data rather than localized prose. Native permission copy belongs in copy/native_en.json and notification templates belong in copy/notifications_en.json; generated consumers must stay in sync. Inline technical identifiers, test fixtures, and user-authored content require narrow documented exemptions rather than broad folders.
sunset_when: Never; copy ownership and locale-safe rendering are permanent product architecture

COPY-VERB-INTEGRITY-001.title: User-facing copy is golden-tested and survives automated renames
COPY-VERB-INTEGRITY-001.instruction: High-traffic user-facing string surfaces — onboarding, booking/CTA flows, dashboard, event detail, chat, notifications, and catalog copy under contracts/ — must be covered by a copy snapshot/golden test. Any automated find-replace rename that can touch prose (e.g. run->event) must re-run the copy golden AND include a human review of the changed string literals before merge. Do not add goldens for every string in the app; the scope is the named high-traffic surfaces plus any string that has previously regressed.
sunset_when: A copy golden covers the high-traffic user-facing string surfaces
Two consecutive renames pass the golden with no manual copy fixes

IMAGE-NETWORK-PRIMITIVE-001.title: Network images go through a shared caching/decode-sized primitive
IMAGE-NETWORK-PRIMITIVE-001.instruction: Remote images must render through a shared CatchNetworkImage primitive providing caching, cacheWidth/cacheHeight decode sizing, a branded errorBuilder, and a loading placeholder. Raw Image.network in feature code is forbidden.
sunset_when: catch_ui_lints flags raw Image.network outside the primitive
All feature image sites route through CatchNetworkImage for three passes

sunset_review.note: raw network image scanner is active; rule stays active until feature image sites consistently route through CatchNetworkImage

FN-IDEMP-001.title: At-least-once triggers/webhooks guard non-idempotent side effects with an event receipt
FN-IDEMP-001.instruction: Every onDocument*/onObject* trigger and payment webhook must guard non-idempotent side effects (push, auto-id flag writes, counter increments) with a deterministic functionEventReceipts receipt (tx.create, no-op on already-exists). Extract and reuse a shared withEventReceipt() helper; id-deterministic durable writes need no guard.
sunset_when: A shared withEventReceipt primitive wraps every at-least-once handler's side effects
Redelivery regression tests exist for each trigger/webhook

FN-DATA-001.title: Backend-owned denormalized counts are mutated inside the source-read transaction
FN-DATA-001.instruction: Denormalized aggregates (event check-in/booked/gender counts, club memberCount, invite paidCount, match unread) must be read and mutated inside the same db.runTransaction, with the +/-1 conditioned on the observed in-transaction state transition. Non-transactional read-modify-write and batch.set overwrites of shared counts are forbidden.
sunset_when: No non-transactional count mutation remains
A projection/count drift check runs in CI or a data-audit job

FN-PAY-001.title: Every capturable payment has a server webhook + reconciliation sweep and a distinct non-recoverable refund state
FN-PAY-001.instruction: Each payment provider must have a server-side webhook (signature-verified) plus a scheduled reconciliation sweep; a 'pending' payment doc must be written at order creation keyed by orderId; a failed refund must be recorded with a distinct terminal status (needs_manual_refund) and a structured alert/metric, never silently as 'completed'.
sunset_when: A Razorpay webhook + reconciliation sweep exist mirroring Stripe
refund_failed is a distinct, alerted terminal state

FN-RATE-001.title: Every checkRateLimit action has an explicit RATE_LIMITS entry; missing entries warn
FN-RATE-001.instruction: Every action string passed to checkRateLimit must have an explicit RATE_LIMITS entry. checkRateLimit must logger.warn when it falls back to DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT without an explicit config so silent loose limits are visible. demoOps and other privileged fan-out callables must declare dedicated limits.
sunset_when: checkRateLimit warns on default fallback
All callables have explicit limits for three passes

FN-AUTHZ-001.title: Client-supplied ids and storage paths are ownership/format-validated before use as keys or action targets
FN-AUTHZ-001.instruction: Client-supplied identifiers and storage paths must be ownership- and format-validated before being used as document ids, canonical URLs/slugs, or admin-SDK action targets (e.g. profile photo storagePath must be under users/{uid}/). Never trust a client id as a deletion target or public path.
sunset_when: A scan flags client-id/path use as keys/targets without validation
Storage-path and doc-id validation cover all sinks for three passes

FN-MOD-001.title: Content moderation runs at the write boundary and covers every delivery channel and public surface
FN-MOD-001.instruction: Moderation must apply at the write boundary so the stored doc, the denormalized preview (lastMessagePreview), and every delivery channel (FCM push) never carry blocked content. Public-rendered free text (reviews, reviewer names, host responses) must be moderated; no surface may bypass it.
sunset_when: Blocked content cannot reach push/preview/public surfaces
Moderation coverage has tests for each channel

FN-PERF-001.title: Aggregation triggers use a before/after diff guard and bound their queries
FN-PERF-001.instruction: Triggers that recompute aggregates must early-return when the relevant fields are unchanged (before/after diff) and must bound their queries (count() aggregations, limits, or incremental deltas) instead of scanning entire collections/lifetime history on every write.
sunset_when: Hot aggregation triggers diff-guard and bound queries
No unbounded full-collection scan on a per-write hot path

FN-INPUT-001.title: User free-text inputs are length-bounded and outputs never leak PII or raw upstream detail
FN-INPUT-001.instruction: All user free-text inputs (reviews, occupation/company, names) must be length-bounded server-side; logs and client errors must not leak PII or raw upstream provider error bodies (e.g. Google Places error.message forwarded verbatim).
sunset_when: All free-text inputs have server-side max lengths
No raw upstream/PII detail reaches clients or logs

FN-DOC-001.title: functions/README.md inventory is CI-diffed against src/index.ts exports
FN-DOC-001.instruction: The canonical functions/README.md function inventory must stay in sync with the actual exports in src/index.ts. Add a CI check that diffs the documented inventory against exports.
sunset_when: A CI check diffs README inventory vs index.ts exports
Inventory stays current for three passes

FN-DUP-001.title: Shared authorization/eligibility/state-machine logic lives in one module
FN-DUP-001.instruction: Authorization, eligibility, attendance, and state-machine logic must live in one shared module, not be copy-pasted across sibling feature modules where copies can diverge into bugs.
sunset_when: Duplicated authz/eligibility logic is consolidated
A duplication scan stays clean for three passes

APP-TARGET-IDENTITY-001.title: Installable app identity resolves from one checked target contract
APP-TARGET-IDENTITY-001.instruction: Consumer and Host are separate installable products. Entrypoints, native ids and capabilities, Firebase registrations, links, force-update keys, routine release ownership, and enabled release-critical callable dependencies must match checked target contracts. A production client feature cannot be enabled until its callable export and live Firebase callable/IAM reachability pass the release preflight; temporary release exceptions require a stable debt id and explicit removal proof.
sunset_when: The app-target contract generates every native and release surface without duplicated identity values
Both products have three consecutive complete release receipts

sunset_review.note: the target scanner now exists, but native and release identity values remain duplicated across generated and hand-maintained surfaces and neither product has three complete release receipts

DESIGN-LEXICON-001.title: Cross-stack design-system implementations map to one checked component lexicon
DESIGN-LEXICON-001.instruction: Every declared component surface symbol must exist in its implementation owner. Surface registry entries marked lexicon=true must reference a real catch.components id and exactly match that contract's surface symbol. Adopted cross-stack form primitives must declare a valid Flutter-owned interaction mode, while each stack retains its native implementation.
sunset_when: Catch no longer ships more than one UI implementation stack

WEB-CALLABLE-VALIDATION-001.title: Admin callable requests and development responses are schema-validated
WEB-CALLABLE-VALIDATION-001.instruction: Every callable used by adminApi must have a generated top-level validator; schema-backed requests validate on every live call and schema-backed responses validate in development or when VITE_ADMIN_VALIDATE_RESPONSES is enabled. Generator drift must fail pretypecheck.
sunset_when: Admin no longer calls Firebase callable functions directly

REPO-ROOT-HYGIENE-001.title: Repository-root entries have explicit owners and safe cleanup contracts
REPO-ROOT-HYGIENE-001.instruction: Every root entry must match exactly one manifest classification. Cleanup is dry-run first and mutates only an explicit allowlisted scope; tracked files, protected paths, worktrees, symlinks, path escapes, and machine-local Markdown links are rejected.
sunset_when: Repository ownership and cleanup safety are enforced by a replacement platform

sunset_review.note: The scanner now enforces the policy; tool availability is evidence of automation, not a reason to remove repository ownership rules.

ROUTE-STRING-001.title: Navigation outside routing config uses named routes or constants
ROUTE-STRING-001.instruction: Production navigation outside lib/routing must use named routes, route constants, or typed route helpers instead of raw path string literals. Raw paths stay owned by route definitions and URL contract files.
sunset_when: Raw route literal scanner stays clean for three app architecture passes

sunset_review.note: route literal scanner is active; rule stays active until raw route literals remain clean for consecutive app architecture passes

WEB-BUNDLE-001.title: Admin route boundaries preserve production bundle budgets
WEB-BUNDLE-001.instruction: Keep data/query controllers behind lazy route boundaries and fail the production build when the JavaScript entry or largest async chunk exceeds the reviewed byte budget.
sunset_when: A replacement performance-budget service enforces equivalent route and entry limits

WEB-CONTROLLER-TEST-001.title: React controller coverage uses explicit behavior-test targets
WEB-CONTROLLER-TEST-001.instruction: Classify every React feature controller or mutation hook in the target manifest. Promote behavior-critical hooks to required with a named importing test suite; use planned for an explicit coverage queue and exempt only for behavior-free compatibility re-exports with a reason. Keep aggregate percentage reports informational.
sunset_when: A replacement mutation/controller contract system enforces equivalent named behavior coverage

WEB-STORYBOOK-BUNDLE-001.title: Admin Storybook preserves app-authored feature chunk boundaries
WEB-STORYBOOK-BUNDLE-001.instruction: Keep Catch-authored admin feature and route-story chunks within the reviewed Storybook budgets. Split feature code and large generated datasets with named Rollup boundaries; do not hide regressions by raising the general Vite warning limit.
sunset_when: A hosted visual review service enforces equivalent per-story application bundle budgets

WEB-FEATURE-SIZE-001.title: Admin feature UI remains decomposed into reviewable modules
WEB-FEATURE-SIZE-001.instruction: Keep route/workspace entries below 900 lines and feature-private panel modules below 1200 lines. Split by cohesive workflow before raising a ceiling; do not bypass the admin feature-export boundary with reusable-looking feature exports.
sunset_when: A structural complexity gate supersedes both reviewed line ceilings with equally deterministic module-boundary enforcement

OPERATIONS-PLATFORM-001.title: Durable business workflows stay outside repository tooling
OPERATIONS-PLATFORM-001.instruction: Resumable runs, work items, leases, budgets, decisions, receipts, and learning state belong in operations/. Legacy tool folders remain compatibility producers. Every workflow manifest stays bound to its executable commands, ordered states, sources, capabilities, and capacity. Shadow mode fails closed. The admin projection crosses into Firestore only through the dry-run-first trusted importer, and publication requires separately authorized execution.
sunset_when: A stronger package-boundary mechanism enforces equivalent placement and authority rules

sunset_review.note: The workflow platform is new and must remain shadow-safe while additional admin flows are added.

ORGANIZER-AUTHORITY-001.title: Organizer is the canonical entity and club is a subtype
ORGANIZER-AUTHORITY-001.instruction: Use organizers, organizerId, organizer relationships, organizer callables, organizer media paths, and organizer product copy as the canonical authority. Club is allowed as organizerType club, real-world content, or an explicitly documented released-client compatibility projection only.
sunset_when: Organizer is replaced by a separately approved canonical entity model

sunset_review.note: The local cutover is implemented but remote parity and legacy retirement are still pending.

PUBLIC-SURFACE-BEHAVIOR-001.title: Keep public viewer and listing authority behavior explicit across app and web
PUBLIC-SURFACE-BEHAVIOR-001.instruction: Treat design/public_surface_behavior.json as the executable decision table for public app and marketing surfaces. Keep viewer-session resolution, app role, profile readiness, viewer relationship, app and web publication, organizer ownership/claim/verification, host/contact identity, event supply/lifecycle/availability/payment, location, independent claim/review capabilities, and their runtimes explicit. Every consumer route must have exactly one matrix owner, every configuration must be verified by the declared app or website harness, and excluded dimension values require a rationale. Public actions fail closed when relationship or runtime authority is unresolved.
sunset_when: Never; cross-surface public behavior remains a permanent product and architecture contract

WEB-VISUAL-001.title: Registry-ready React stories have deterministic visual baselines
WEB-VISUAL-001.instruction: Every Storybook registry entry marked ready must resolve to a built story and match committed desktop and mobile baselines for the current operating system with reduced motion, loaded fonts, and the configured pixel tolerance. Never compare Darwin-authored system-ui pixels against Linux; blocking CI uses reviewed Ubuntu 24.04 baselines and retains actual plus diff artifacts on failure.
sunset_when: A replacement visual-regression service enforces the same registry-backed coverage

sunset_review.note: The local visual gate remains necessary until a replacement service covers the same registry-backed desktop and mobile states.
